# Publishing Client Applications Generated by CDD-Swift

Once `cdd-swift from_openapi to_sdk` creates a fully-fledged Swift client library for your platform, it is often required to keep this artifact up to date automatically or publish it via a Package registry.

## Automatic Github Actions Sync
A standard approach is to use a GitHub Action in a "cronjob" fashion to sync the SDK code from the server's OpenAPI specification dynamically.

```yaml
name: Sync Swift SDK

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 0 * * *' # Daily
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  sync:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Fetch OpenAPI Spec
        run: curl -s https://api.myserver.com/openapi.json -o spec.json
      - name: Generate SDK
        run: cdd-swift from_openapi to_sdk -i spec.json -o MySDK
      - name: Push to Branch
        uses: peter-evans/create-pull-request@v6
        with:
          branch: chore-update-sdk
          title: "chore(sdk): Sync OpenAPI Definition"
```

## Distributing the Generated Swift Package
Since the output is an automatically scaffolded `Package.swift` file, the typical distribution pipeline for a Swift package is via Source Control natively (GitHub).

To deploy:
1. Push the generated `MySDK` directory to a new GitHub repository (e.g. `github.com/my-org/MySDK-swift`).
2. Add a Git Tag: `git tag 1.0.0 && git push origin 1.0.0`
3. Consumers can instantly use it via Swift Package Manager (SPM):
```swift
dependencies: [
    .package(url: "https://github.com/my-org/MySDK-swift.git", from: "1.0.0")
]
```
